rulings.Case Title: KIERAN BUCKLEY et al VS. DAVID BARTENWERFER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Futher Discovery Responses To Defts David And Kate Bartenwerfer'S Responses To Special Interrogas Sets Five And Six; Request For Production Of Doucments Set Trhee; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS KIERAN BUCKLEY, VERONICA CAMPBELL Motion To Compel Futher Discovery Responses To Defendants David And Kate Bartenwerfer's Responses To Special Interrogatories Sets Five And Six; Request For Production Of Doucments Set Three; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BYKHOVSKY et al VS. WILLIAM PORTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT WILLIAM WALSH, JEFF CARVELL'S Special Motion To Strike is Denied. While the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is subject to CCP 425.16 because it pleads non-incidental protected activity, cross-complainant has demonstrated a viable cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty based on non-privileged conduct that is outside the scope of the litigation privilege. Cross-defendants' request to strike paragraph 31 of the cross complaint is denied. Cross-complainant's request for fees and costs is denied because the motion is not frivolous. rulings.Case Title: TRICO PIPES, A LABOR-MANAGEMENT COOPERATION VS. BIG BEARS CONSTRUCTION LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants San Ramon Valley Unified School District Et. Al. Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 23 - PART TWO OF TENTATIVE - Defendants San Ramon Valley Unified School District Et. Al. Motion For Summary Adjudication - The argument that plaintiff is not an agent of the Big Bear employees is irrelevant for the same reason. However, even if the assignments were invalid, plaintiff raised triable issues whether it is the Big Bear employees' agent in serving and enforcing the stop notices and payment bonds. (Lawson Declaration. 4-8; Assignor Declarations 2-12). Plaintiff is exempt from the requirements of Civil Code 3098(c) and 3252(a) as the assignee or agent of the employees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: TRICO PIPES, A LABOR-MANAGEMENT COOPERATION VS. BIG BEARS CONSTRUCTION LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants San Ramon Valley Unified School District, Liberty Mutual Insurance Company, Sausal Corporation, City Of Emeryville And Evergreen School District'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 23, Defendants San Ramon Valley Unified School District, Liberty Mutual Insurance Company, Sausal Corporation, City Of Emeryville And Evergreen School District'S Motion For Summary Adjudication IS Denied in its entirety. While plaintiff is not a "laborer" under Civil Code 3089(b) because it has not presented evidence of any agreement that a portion of the Big Bear employees' wages would be paid to plaintiff, plaintiff has standing to assert claims against the moving defendants pursuant to the assignments signed by the Big Bear employees in September 2010. Plaintiff's actions are not ultra vires. Enforcing the prevailing wage laws as plaintiff attempts to do here serves the purposes enumerated in the Trust Agreement through which it was created. 29 U.S.C. 186 is not violated because neither the Northern California Mechanical Association nor the Steamfitters Local Union No. 159 instructs plaintiff as to its job duties or directs the work plaintiff performs. Defendants have not presented any evidence that the decision to pursue the assignments or this action was directed by or on behalf of the Northern California Mechanical Association. Notwithstanding the holdings in Mills v. La Verne Land Co. (1893) 97 Cal. 254, 255 and McCrea v. Johnson (1894) 104 Cal. 224, 225, the right to serve the stop notice is assignable. Those cases pertained to a different statutory scheme and stop notice statutes are to be liberally construed in accordance with the public policy to protect the compensation of laborers on works of improvement. Labor Code 300 does not apply because the Big Bear employees do not belong to the class of persons the statute was designed to protect. The argument that plaintiff is not an authorized stop notice or payment bond claimant is irrelevant once it is determined that the assignments are valid.

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GOTTLIEB et al VS. SCOTT COX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT COX, SCOTT INDIVIDUALLY AND AS A TRUSTEE OF THE, COX, JOYCE INDIVIDUALLY AND AS A TRUSTEE OF THE AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Denied. Even assuming that the plaintiffs' claims arise out of protected conduct, plaintiffs have demonstrated the requisite minimal level of merit to those claims. Plaintiffs' request for fees and costs is denied because the motion is not frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: HILAIRE LOUIS DUFRESNE III VS. YEHUDA GOLANI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT YEHUDA GOLANI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. The first amended complaint identifies the prongs on which the 17200 claim is based, as well as alleges ultimate facts with sufficient particularity. That plaintiff's injury is personal is of no matter. Defendant's alleged actions as a landlord constitute an unfair business practice. Plaintiff has stated a viable CCP 1060 claim. Although injunctive relief is not technically a cause of action, plaintiff has pled sufficient facts entitling him to such relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: HILAIRE LOUIS DUFRESNE III VS. YEHUDA GOLANI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 11, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT YEHUDA GOLANI MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. The rent received by defendant from plaintiff is money allegedly obtained in violation of the unfair competition law. Therefore, the prayer for disgorgement of profits is proper as a form of restitution. Plaintiff has stated a proper claim for attorney's fees under the statutes referenced in the first amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = 302/hk
